Title: Evaluation of an Online Fatigue Self-management Group Intervention
for Adults with Multiple Sclerosis
“This 7-week intervention will follow the published protocol outlined in Reported elements: Content: quickly described but referenced
Managing Fatigue (Packer et al., 1995). overall duration: six weeks
Full participation requires 2 hours per week of online contribution. number: six sessions
Each session is highly structured and includes an education session, frequency: weekly
practice activities, discussions and homework assignment. The topics duration of sessions: two hours
include the importance of rest, communication and body mechanics, mode of delivery: online
organizing work stations, setting priorities and standards, balancing standardization methods: highly structured sessions
schedules and setting goals. All teaching content, worksheets, and care provider's qualification: occupational therapists, nurses or
homework assignments are provided online. Also participants can share social workers trained for two days
information, express their ideas or feelings and offer advice or support Missing elements:
to one another. none
